elements.answer1:
Baptism and communion are the two great signs that Jesus gave to his church. They are closely related: both are commanded by Jesus, both are signs of something spiritually real, and both are connected to belonging to God’s family.

The normal pattern — in the New Testament and across most of church history — is that a person is baptised first, and then begins taking communion as part of the gathered church. Baptism marks entry into God’s family; communion marks continuing in it. If baptism is the door, communion is the ongoing meal at the family table.

If you have been taking communion before being baptised, you have not committed a grave sin — but this study would encourage you to get baptised. The two belong together, and in the right order.

elements.term1: Do I have to be baptised in front of the church?
elements.question1: Is a public baptism — in front of the gathered congregation — strictly necessary?
elements.answer1:
The Bible does not prescribe a specific minimum audience for baptism. But the nature of baptism — as a public declaration, a witness to the gospel, and an act of entering the community of God’s people — means that doing it privately would miss much of its meaning.

Baptism is not a personal religious experience to be kept quiet. It is an announcement. It says to the watching world: this is what Jesus has done for me. The church wants to witness it — not as passive spectators, but as the family receiving a new member. For them, witnessing a baptism is an encouragement, a reminder of the gospel, and a moment of shared joy.

If there are specific circumstances that make a fully public baptism complicated — safety concerns, family opposition, or other genuine difficulties — speak with your church leader. Wisdom applies in particular situations. But the default, and the strongly preferred pattern, is: in front of the church, together, as a family.

elements.term1: My parents don’t want me to be baptised — what should I do?
elements.question1: How do you handle the situation where the people closest to you are opposed to your baptism?
elements.answer1:
With patience, honesty, and wisdom — and without making it a battle.

Start by making sure your parents understand what baptism actually is and why you want to do it. Much opposition comes from misunderstanding — a fear that you are joining a cult, rejecting your family’s identity, or being reckless. Explain clearly: this is a declaration of your personal faith in Jesus Christ, an act of obedience to him, and a step you are taking because you believe it is right.

Ask your church leader to speak with your parents if they are willing. Continue to pray — consistently and specifically — for God to change their hearts or give you wisdom.

If you are young and still under your parents’ authority, it may be wise to wait for a season while continuing to pray and explain. Honouring your parents is also a command of God. Hold both commands with care, guided by your church leader.

If you are an adult, and you have been clear and patient in your explanations, you can go ahead — gently, lovingly, without making it a confrontation. Your ultimate obedience is to Jesus. But do everything you can to help your family understand before you take that step.

elements.term1: Is there a good reason to not be baptised?
elements.question1: Are there any circumstances in which it would be right for someone not to be baptised — at least for now?
elements.answer1:
Yes — there are a small number of legitimate reasons to pause or delay.

The most important is this: if the person does not yet genuinely believe in Jesus Christ. Baptising someone who is not a Christian gives false assurance and misrepresents what baptism is. If you are still unsure about your own faith, be honest about that with your church leader — and keep working through it — rather than being baptised before you are ready.

A second reason is if you do not yet understand what baptism means. This is not a reason to never be baptised — and it should not become a permanent excuse — but genuine understanding matters. Hopefully this study has helped. If questions remain, keep talking with your church leader.

A third reason is if you are being pressured into it — by parents, church leaders, or friends — without it being your own genuine, personal desire. Baptism must be an act of faith, not an act of social compliance.

These are the legitimate reasons. Everything else — fear, laziness, embarrassment, busyness, vague intention — is not a good reason. The command to be baptised comes from Jesus Christ, the King of the universe and the most loving person who has ever lived. He commands it. His people should want to obey him.

elements.term1: At what age should a young person be baptised?
elements.question1: Is there a minimum age for baptism — and how do you know if a young person’s faith is genuine enough?
elements.answer1:
The Bible does not specify an age. It specifies a condition: credible, personal faith in Jesus Christ.

The key questions for a young person are not about age but about authenticity. Is this faith genuinely their own — or is it inherited from parents, performed for approval, or driven by peer pressure? Does the child understand what they are saying yes to? Can they explain, in their own words, what Jesus has done and why they trust him?

It can also help to look for evidence that their faith has been tested in some way. Have they had to speak up for Jesus among friends, at school, or in their community? Have they kept trusting God through a hard season in their life? A faith that has cost something is more likely to be real.

Church leaders should affirm genuine faith in children warmly — not create unnecessary doubt or suggest they wait simply because of their age. But they should also be wise guides. If there is genuine uncertainty about whether the faith is the child’s own, it may be appropriate to suggest waiting a little longer. And if it is clearly real — if the child knows what they believe and why, and their faith has been visible and costly — then age alone is not a barrier.

elements.instruction1: As you reach the end of this study, take a moment to reflect on where you have arrived. Choose the option that best reflects how you feel right now.
statements1: I now have a clear and confident understanding of what baptism is and what it means.
I am sure that I have personally put my faith in Jesus Christ and that baptism is right for me.
I have made — or am ready to make — a firm decision to be baptised.
I have my baptism notes in order and am ready to finalise the details with my church leader.
I am approaching my baptism with joy — not just as an obligation, but as something I genuinely want to do.

elements.text1: A Final Reflection

elements.question1: Look back at the question you wrote at the very beginning of this study — the main question you were hoping it would answer. Has it been answered? What has changed in how you think about baptism since you began?

Is there anything that remains unresolved — a question, a hesitation, or a concern — that you still need to bring to your church leader before your baptism? Write it down here so you don’t forget to raise it.
Write a short prayer — in your own words — about your baptism. Thank God for what he has done for you. Ask him for what you need. Tell him what you are looking forward to.

elements.text1:
This is the end of the study.

What comes next is not in a book. It is in the water. It is in the faces of the people watching. It is in the life that follows — the ordinary, faithful, sometimes stumbling, always-held life of a person who belongs to Jesus and to his people.

May the Lord bless you in your baptism.
And may he get all the glory.

Amen.

studyOnboardingSlides.body1:
This study will help you understand who baptism is for, what it is all about, and whether it is something you should do.

Work through each section at your own pace alongside a church leader who will help you think it through.
studyOnboardingSlides.body2:
Wannan nazarin zai taimake ka fahimtar wa baftisma yake gare shi, abin da ke cikinta, da kuma ko shi ne abin da ya kamata ka yi.

Karanta kowane ɓangare a kan lokacinka tare da shugaban coci wanda zai taimake ka tunani game da shi.
studyOnboardingSlides.body3:
Janngirde nde ɗon wallitoo ma anndugo ko bomɓo batiisma waɗi, ɗume ɗum fof ɓuri, e so ɗum ko huunde a haani waɗude.

Janngu kooɗume nokku dow sa’a ma tawa bee ganndo laaɓal ngal wallitoo ma miijitugo dow ɗum.

studyOnboardingSlides.body1:
Baptism is for people who have heard the good news of Jesus, repented of their sin, and put their trust in him.

It is not for perfect people — it is for forgiven people who now want to live lives that please God. Acts 2:36–41 shows us that baptism followed straight after believing.
studyOnboardingSlides.body2:
Baftisma yana gare mutanen da suka ji labarin mai dadi na Yesu, suka tuba daga zunubansu, suka kuma dogara gare shi.

Ba don mutane cikakku ba ne — yana don mutanen da aka yafe musu zunubi waɗanda yanzu suke son rayuwa da take faranta wa Allah rai. Ayyukan 2:36–41 yana nuna mana cewa baftisma ya biyo bayan imani nan take.
studyOnboardingSlides.body3:
Batiisma waɗi bomɓo nanii khabaaru moƴƴo Yeesu, toobi dow juuɓe muɓɓen, nden tawii dow makko.

Waɗaa ngam yimɓe fuu timmunɓe — waɗi ngam yimɓe ɓe mpaamaama juuɓe muɓɓen ɓe ŋaaɗii yewtude ngendam nde juutoo Allah. Kuuɗe 2:36–41 hollitiima en batiisma ɗon wari caggal tawagol taccagol.

studyOnboardingSlides.body1: Baptism is a visible sign of three things Jesus has done for you: a bath — your sins washed away; a burial — your old life dead and gone; and a birth — you raised into new life in Christ. Romans 6:1–14 shows that being baptised by full immersion is a picture of dying and rising with Jesus.
studyOnboardingSlides.body2: Baftisma alama ce ta gani ta abubuwa uku da Yesu ya yi dominku: wanka — an wanke zunubanka; jana’iza — rayuwarka ta tsoho ta mutu kuma ta ɓace; da kuma haihuwa — an tashe ka zuwa sabuwar rayuwa a cikin Almasihu. Romawa 6:1–14 yana nuna cewa an nutse gaba ɗaya a cikin ruwa alama ce ta mutuwa da tashin hankali tare da Yesu.
studyOnboardingSlides.body3: Batiisma ko alaamat jeen keewɗe nde yi’etee ngam huunde tati Yeesu waɗannoo ma: lootaari — juuɓe ma lootiima; deeƴre — ngendam ma beli feƴƴiti e yahii; e jibinannde — a tawtinaama ngendam pamɗam nder Almasiihu. Romanko’en 6:1–14 hollitiima batiisma bee njiimuɗam fof nder ndiyam ko seedantaare maatugol maayde e toownaade bee Yeesu.

studyOnboardingSlides.body1:
When you are baptised you are publicly declaring that you now belong to God and to his people, the church.

Jesus commanded his disciples to go and make disciples of all nations, baptising them in the name of the Father, Son and Holy Spirit (Matthew 28:18–20). Joining a local church is how you grow, serve, and keep going.
studyOnboardingSlides.body2:
Lokacin da aka yi maka baftisma kana bayyana wa jama’a cewa yanzu kana cikin Allah da mutanensa, cocin.

Yesu ya umarci almajiransa su je su yi almajirai a cikin al’ummai duka, suna yi musu baftisma da suna Uba, Da, da Ruhu Mai Tsarki (Matiyu 28:18–20). Shiga cocin gida-gida shi ne yadda kake girma, yin hidima, da kuma ci gaba.
studyOnboardingSlides.body3:
Wakkati a batiisaama a seedantake yeeso yimɓe wonde hannde a won nder Allah e yimɓe makko, laaɓal ngal.

Yeesu noddinii almuɓɓe makko ngam yahde e waɗugo almuɓɓe nder leƴƴi fof, batiisatooɓe ɓe e innde Baaba, Ɓii e Ruuhu Potal (Matta 28:18–20). Naatugol nder laaɓal gure ko no a heɓortoo battanere, wa’ata kuugal, e juutna yahdu.

studyOnboardingSlides.body1: Once you have decided to be baptised, there are practical steps to work through with your church leader: fixing a date and place, deciding who to invite, choosing what to wear, and preparing what you will say — your testimony of what Jesus has done for you. Tap ⓘ beside any Bible reference to read the passage without leaving the app.
studyOnboardingSlides.body2: Da zarar ka yanke shawarar a yi maka baftisma, akwai matakai na aiki da za ka bi tare da shugaban cocinku: saita kwanan wata da wuri, yanke shawara game da wa za ka gayyata, zabar abin da za ka sa, da kuma shirya abin da za ka faɗa — shahadarku ta abin da Yesu ya yi dominku. Taɓa ⓘ kusa da kowane bayanin nassi don karanta nassin ba tare da barin manhajar ba.
studyOnboardingSlides.body3: Caggal ma yantirii batiisaade, gaa huɗɗe goɗɗe kuuɗe ngenndam hawrunɗe bee ganndo laaɓal ngal: siftorgo datal e nokku, humpitaago ko bomɓo noddataa, suɓugo ko waɗataa, e heɓtoraago ko a wownitaa — seede ma dow ɗume Yeesu waɗannoo ma. Mem ⓘ ɓadi kooɗume nassi ngam janngugo nassi on tawa a urtaayi nder manhaja nden.

sections.heading2: Don Me Aka Yi Wannan Nazarin?

blocks.title1: People who have recently become Christians
blocks.title2: Mutanen da kwanan nan suka zama Kiristoci
blocks.body1: If you have recently put your trust in Jesus Christ and want to understand what baptism is and whether it is for you, this study is written for you. It works through three Bible passages that explain who baptism is for, what it means, and how it connects you to God’s people.
blocks.body2: Idan kwanan nan ka dogara da Yesu Almasihu kuma kana son fahimtar abin da baftisma yake da kuma ko yana gare ka, wannan nazarin an rubuta shi dominku. Yana duba nassosi uku na Littafi Mai Tsarki da ke bayyana wa baftisma yake gare shi, abin da yake nufi, da kuma yadda yake haɗa ka da mutanen Allah.
blocks.body3: To a taccii Yeesu Almasiihu hannde nden a yiɗi anndugo ko batiisma woni e so waɗi ngam ma, janngirde nde ɗon winndaa ngam ma. Ɗum ɗon wolwa dow nassiije tati Linjiila ɗe fassirta ko bomɓo batiisma waɗi, ko ɗum hollitta, e no ɗum gasa ma bee yimɓe Allah.

blocks.title2: Mutanen da suke da tambayoyi game da baftisma
blocks.body1: You may have grown up in a church but never been baptised yourself, or you may have been baptised as a baby and are wondering whether that counts. This study does not assume any prior knowledge. It starts from the beginning and works through what the Bible actually says, with space to ask your own questions along the way.
blocks.body2: Watakila ka girma a coci amma ba a taɓa yi maka baftisma ba, ko kuma an yi maka baftisma a lokacin yarinta kuma kana mamaki ko hakan ya isa. Wannan nazarin baya ɗauka cewa kana da wani ilimi na baya. Yana fara daga farko kuma yana duba abin da Littafi Mai Tsarki ke faɗa hakika, tare da damar yin tambayoyinka a kan hanya.
blocks.body3: Waɗii a janngi nder laaɓal kono a batiisaaka hoore ma, koo batiisaama to piilugol e ɗon miijitii so ɗum moƴƴi. Janngirde nde tammaaki a mari anndal timmuɗam aran. Ɗum fuɗɗorta fuɗɗorde nden wolwa dow ko Linjiila wi’i tawaaɗum, bee nokkuure ngam ƴamugo ƴameeje ma e laawol.

blocks.body1: This study is suitable for young people who are considering baptism, provided a church leader and, where possible, your parents are involved. Faith is your own, and so is this decision — but it is not one to make alone. The book gives specific guidance on how to think through baptism with your parents if they have questions or concerns.
blocks.body2: Wannan nazarin ya dace da matasa da ke tunanin baftisma, muddin shugaban coci da, idan zai yiwu, iyayenka suna ciki. Imani naka ne, haka kuma wannan shawara — amma ba ita ce za a yanke ita kaɗai ba. Littafin yana ba da jagoranci na musamman kan yadda za ka yi tunani game da baftisma tare da iyayenka idan suna da tambayoyi ko damuwa.
blocks.body3: Janngirde nde moƴƴi ngam sukaaɓe miijiiɓe batiisma, so ganndo laaɓal e, haa ɗume woni, maaɓe on ngoni e dow. Tawagol woni ta ma, hono ɗum yantirgol on fuu — kono ɗum moƴƴaani yantugo tan hoore ma. Deftere nde hollitiima jagoramol feere dow no miijirtaa batiisma bee maaɓe to ɓe mari ƴameeje koo haaleele.

blocks.title1: Christians who want to understand baptism more deeply
blocks.title2: Kiristoci da ke son fahimtar baftisma sosai
blocks.body1: Even if you were baptised some time ago, working through this study can deepen your understanding of what your baptism meant and still means. The three Bible passages — Acts 2, Romans 6, and Matthew 28 — open up the full weight of what it means to be united with Christ, washed clean, and joined to his people.
blocks.body2: Ko da an yi maka baftisma lokaci da ya wuce, nazarin wannan zai iya ƙara zurfafa fahimtarka game da abin da baftismarka ya nufawa da kuma har yanzu yana nufi. Nassosi uku na Littafi Mai Tsarki — Ayyuka 2, Romawa 6, da Matiyu 28 — suna buɗe cikakken nauyin abin da yake nufi a haɗa da Almasihu, a wankewa, da kuma a shiga cikin mutanensa.
blocks.body3: Koo batiisaama ɗoon hawriri, waɗugo janngirde nde ɗon ɓeydugo anndugo ko batiisma ma hollitii e ko ɗum ɗon hollita hannde. Nassiije tati Linjiila ɗee — Kuuɗe 2, Romanko’en 6, e Matta 28 — ɗon yiltina teddeendi timmiindi ko fassirtaa laataago nguuɗo e Almasiihu, lotaado, e naataago nder yimɓe makko.

sections.heading2: Yadda Ake Amfani Da Wannan Nazarin

blocks.title1: Work through it with a church leader
blocks.title2: Yi shi tare da shugaban coci
blocks.body1: This study is designed to be worked through alongside a church leader. You can read each section on your own first and write your answers, then meet with your church leader to talk through what you found. Or you can read and discuss it together from the start. Either way, your church leader is there to help you understand, answer your questions, and guide you towards your baptism.
blocks.body2: An tsara wannan nazarin ne don a yi shi tare da shugaban coci. Kana iya karanta kowane ɓangare da kanka da farko ka rubuta amsoshinka, sannan ka haɗu da shugaban cocinku don tattauna abin da ka gano. Ko kuma kuna iya karanta shi tare da tattauna shi tun farko. Ko ta yaya, shugaban cocinku yana nan don taimaka maka fahimta, amsa tambayoyinka, da kuma jagorantarka zuwa ga baftismarka.
blocks.body3: Janngirde nde ɗon winndaa ngam waɗugo bee ganndo laaɓal. A waɗan janngugo kooɗume tekkudi hoore ma aran nden winnda jaabaawol ma, nden pota bee ganndo laaɓal ngal ngam tattaunago ko a keɓi. Koo on waɗan janngugo e tattaunago fof kawtal fuɗɗorde. Ko waawi, ganndo laaɓal ngal ɗon woni ngam wallitugo ma anndugo, jaabɗo ƴameeje ma, e ardaango ma to batiisma ma.

blocks.body1: Each of the first three sections has a Bible passage to read, questions about that passage to help you look carefully at the text, and space to write your answers. Section four is more practical — it gives you space to plan the details of your baptism. At the back of the study there is also a bonus section that answers common questions people ask.
blocks.body2: Kowane ɓangare daga cikin ɓangarori uku na farko yana da nassin Littafi Mai Tsarki don karanta, tambayoyi game da wannan nassin don taimaka maka duba rubutun da kyau, da kuma sarari don rubuta amsoshinka. Ɓangare na huɗu ya fi zama na aiki — yana ba ka sarari don tsara cikakkun bayanai na baftismarka. A bayan nazarin akwai kuma sashen ƙarin bayani wanda ke amsa tambayoyin da mutane sukan yi.
blocks.body3: Kooɗume tekkudi nder tati araneeje ɗon mari nassi Linjiila ngam janngugo, ƴameeje dow nassi ngon ngam wallitugo ma ndara ruunde e ruunde, e nokkuure ngam winndude jaabaawol ma. Tekkudi nayaɓo on ɓuri laataago ko huutortee — ɗum ɗon hokka ma nokkuure ngam daftaago kuɗɗe batiisma ma. Haa ragare janngirde ɗon woni fuu tekkudi ɓeydingol nde jaabata ƴameeje ɗe yimɓe ɓuri ƴamugo.

blocks.title1: Take your time — but do not put it off
blocks.title2: Yi hakuri — amma kada ka jinkirta
blocks.title3: Hokku hoore ma wakkati — kono taa a ɓoornu
blocks.body1: There is no rush to work through this study, and it is fine to sit with a question that is hard. At the same time, the New Testament consistently shows that people were baptised soon after coming to faith — not because they had all the answers, but because baptism is a command of Jesus and his people want to obey him. The aim of this study is not to delay your baptism but to help you move towards it with confidence.
blocks.body2: Babu gaggawa wajen nazarin wannan, kuma lafiya ne ka zauna da tambayar da ke da wuya. A lokaci guda, Sabon Alkawari yana nuna akai-akai cewa mutane sun yi baftisma ba da dadewa ba bayan sun zo imani — ba don suna da dukkan amsoshi ba, amma saboda baftisma umurnin Yesu ne kuma mutanensa suna son biyayya gare shi. Manufar wannan nazarin ba ita ce jinkirta baftismarka ba amma taimaka maka kusantar da shi da kwarin gwiwa.
blocks.body3: Walaa herwude ngam waɗugo janngirde nde, e moƴƴi siwtude dow ƴamde caɗtunde. Kono e wakkati gooto, Ahdu Pamɗo ɗon hollita wakkati fuu yimɓe batiisaama caggal taccagol law — naa ko ɓe njiimi jaabaaji fof, amma ko batiisma ko Yeesu yamiri nden yimɓe makko ɓon yiɗi jafaago. Ɗabbunde janngirde nde naa ngam ɓoornaago batiisma ma amma ngam wallitugo ma yahde to maajum bee temmingol.

blocks.title1: A guide, not just an examiner
blocks.title2: Mai jagoranci, ba mai jarrabawa kawai ba
blocks.body1:
Your church leader is not there to test whether you have the right answers. They are there to walk alongside you — to explain things that are unclear, to help you think through your own faith, to answer the questions in the bonus section that you want to discuss, and to help you plan and prepare for your baptism.

Use the Share button at the bottom of each section to send your written answers to your church leader before you meet. This gives them a window into your thinking and makes for a much richer conversation.
blocks.body2:
Shugaban cocinku ba yana nan don jarrabawa ko kana da amsoshi daidai ba. Yana nan don tafiya tare da kai — don bayyana abubuwan da ba su bayyana ba, don taimaka maka yin tunani game da imaninka, don amsa tambayoyin da ke sashen ƙarin bayani da kake son tattaunawa, da kuma don taimaka maka tsarawa da shirya baftismarka.

Yi amfani da maballin Raba a ƙasan kowane ɓangare don aika amsoshinka zuwa ga shugaban cocinku kafin ku haɗu. Wannan yana ba su damar ganin tunaninka kuma yana sa tattaunawar ta kasance mai fa’ida sosai.
blocks.body3:
Ganndo laaɓal ngal ɗon wonataa ngam jarabaago so a mari jaabaaji goonga. Ɗon woni ngam yahdugo tawa ma — ngam fassirago huunde moƴƴaani bayinaama, ngam wallitugo ma miijitago tawagol ma, ngam jaabago ƴameeje nder tekkudi ɓeydingol ngo a yiɗi tattaunago, e ngam wallitugo ma daftaago e heɓtoraago batiisma ma.

Naftir buton Yeeyu haa les kooɗume tekkudi ngam neldugo jaabaawol ma winndungol to ganndo laaɓal bako on pota. Ɗum ɗon hokka ɓe laawol yi’ugo numtol ma nden waɗan tattaunawa ndun mawnugo sanne.

blocks.title1: If you are a young person
blocks.body1: If you are a young person thinking about baptism, it is important that your parents are involved in the process. This may not feel easy, but your church leader will know how to help your parents understand what baptism is about and why you want to be baptised. The bonus section at the back of the study also addresses this question directly.
blocks.body2: Idan kai matashi ne da ke tunanin baftisma, yana da muhimmanci cewa iyayenka suna cikin wannan tsari. Wannan na iya zama ba sauƙi ba, amma shugaban cocinku zai san yadda ake taimaka wa iyayenka fahimtar abin da baftisma yake nufi da kuma dalilin da ya sa kake son yin baftisma. Sashen ƙarin bayani a bayan nazarin kuma yana magana akan wannan tambayar kai tsaye.
blocks.body3: To a sukaaɗo miijiiɗo batiisma, ɗum haani maaɓe on ngona e dow laawol ngon. Ɗum waɗaali ɓuri moƴƴude wakkati fuu, kono ganndo laaɓal ngal anndi no wallirta maaɓe anndugo ko batiisma hollitta e ko waɗi a yiɗi batiisaade. Tekkudi ɓeydingol ngol ragare janngirde ɗon fuu wolwa dow ƴamde nde ɗon timmuki.

blocks.body1: Each of the first three sections has two kinds of questions. The passage questions ask you to look carefully at the Bible text — what it actually says, what words mean, and what is happening in the story. The personal questions ask how the passage connects to your own life and faith — where you are now, and what you need to think through.
blocks.body2: Kowane ɓangare daga cikin ɓangarori uku na farko yana da ire-iren tambayoyi biyu. Tambayoyin nassi suna neman ka duba rubutun Littafi Mai Tsarki da kyau — abin da yake faɗa hakika, abin da kalmomi ke nufi, da abin da ke faruwa a labarin. Tambayoyin sirri suna tambayar yadda nassin ke da alaƙa da rayuwarka da imaninka — inda kake yanzu, da kuma abin da kake buƙatar tunani game da shi.
blocks.body3: Kooɗume tekkudi nder tati araneeje ɗon mari irin ƴameeje ɗiɗi. Ƴameeje nassi ɗon ƴama ma ndara ruunde e ruunde dow ruunde Linjiila — ko ɗum wi’i tawaaɗum, ko kelmeeji hollitta, e ko waɗata e tindol ngol. Ƴameeje hoore neɗɗo ɗon ƴama no nassi on gasa bee ngendam ma e tawagol ma — hol to a woni hannde, e ko a haani miijitago.

blocks.title1: You do not need to have all the answers
blocks.title2: Ba lazimi ka sami dukkan amsoshi ba
blocks.body1: The questions are not a test. If you are not sure about something, say so — that is exactly what your church leader is there to help with. The bonus section at the back addresses many of the most common questions people have, including questions about infant baptism, sprinkling versus immersion, whether you need to be baptised to take communion, and what to do if your parents object. If your question is not there, ask your church leader.
blocks.body2: Tambayoyin ba jarabawa ba ne. Idan ba ka da tabbas game da wani abu, faɗa haka — wannan shi ne ainihin abin da shugaban cocinku yake nan don taimakawa. Sashen ƙarin bayani a bayan yana magana game da yawancin tambayoyin da mutane sukan yi, ciki har da tambayoyi game da baftismar jarirai, fesawa da kuma nutsuwa, ko kana buƙatar baftisma don shan Sakaramanti, da kuma abin da za ka yi idan iyayenka sun ƙi. Idan tambayarka ba ta can ba, tambaya shugaban cocinku.
blocks.body3: Ƴameeje ɗee naa jarabaaki on. To a moƴƴaani anndugo dow huunde, wi’u ɗum — ko ɗum timmuki waɗi ganndo laaɓal ngal ngam wallitugo. Tekkudi ɓeydingol ngol ragare ɗon wolwa dow heewi ƴameeje ɗe yimɓe ɓuri ƴamugo, ɗum heewi ƴameeje dow batiisma piilugol, faccude koo njiimuɗam, so haaniima batiisaade ngam naatude nder njaamri, e ko waɗata to maaɓe ngalaa yiɗude. To ƴamde ma walaa ɗoon, ƴam ganndo laaɓal ngal.

leadersNotesData.subtitle2: Bayanan Shugabanni — Yana Tunani Kan Baftisma?
intro1: These notes are designed to help church leaders, pastors, and mentors guide individuals or small groups through this baptism preparation study. Whether you are working one-to-one with a new believer or leading a small group of people preparing for baptism together, these notes will help you get the most out of each chapter — for the people you are leading and for yourself.

Work through the material yourself first. The best baptism preparation leaders are those who have sat with the questions personally before they help others do the same. Even if you were baptised many years ago, revisiting why you were baptised — and what it meant — will make you a far better guide.

Remember that some participants may not yet be Christians. This study begins by asking directly whether the person is a Christian. Do not assume that everyone who comes to baptism preparation has genuinely put their faith in Jesus. Some may be exploring. Some may be going through the motions to please a family member. Handle these possibilities with grace and without pressure — but do not ignore them.

The infant baptism question will almost certainly arise. Handle it with care. People who were baptised as infants may feel that questioning that experience is an attack on their family or their tradition. Acknowledge the genuine theological disagreement that exists among Christians, but be clear about the position this study takes and why. Avoid being dismissive of other traditions.

Create space for honest doubt. Baptism preparation can surface significant fears — fear of public speaking, fear of family reactions, fear of committing publicly to a life they are not sure they can sustain. These fears are real. Make sure participants know they can voice them without being judged or rushed.

Don’t treat this as a checklist to complete. The goal is not to get someone through six chapters as quickly as possible and into the water. The goal is a person who understands what they are doing, is genuinely ready to do it, and will look back on their baptism for the rest of their life as a moment of clarity and joy.
intro2: An tsara waɗannan bayanan ne don taimaka wa shugabannin coci, fastoci, da masu jagoranci su shiryar da mutane ɗaya ko ƙananan ƙungiyoyi ta wannan nazarin shirye-shiryen baftisma. Ko kuna aiki kai tsaye da sabon mai imani ko kuna jagorantar ƙaramin rukunin mutanen da suke shirye-shirye don baftisma tare, waɗannan bayanan za su taimake ku samun fa’ida sosai daga kowane babi.

Yi nazarin kayan aikin da kanka da farko. Mafi kyawun shugabannin shirye-shiryen baftisma su ne waɗanda suka zauna da tambayoyin da kansu kafin su taimaka wa wasu yin hakan. Ko da an yi maka baftisma shekaru da yawa da suka wuce, sake duba dalilin da ya sa aka yi maka baftisma — da abin da ya nufta — zai sa ka zama jagoranci mai kyau sosai.

Ka tuna cewa wasu mahalarta ba Kiristoci ba ne tukuna. Wannan nazarin yana farawa ta hanyar tambaya kai tsaye ko mutumin Kirista ne. Kada ka ɗauka cewa duk wanda ya zo don shirye-shiryen baftisma ya sa imani da gaske a cikin Yesu. Wasu suna iya bincike. Wasu suna iya bin abubuwan da suka saba don faranta ran wani memba na iyali. Yi mu’amala da waɗannan yiwuwar da alheri da kuma ba tare da matsin lamba ba.

Tambayar baftismar jariri za ta taso tabbas. Yi mu’amala da ita da kulawa. Mutanen da aka yi musu baftisma tun suna jarirai na iya ji cewa tambayar wannan kwarewar kai hari ne ga iyalinsu ko al’adarsu. Ka yarda da sabani na gaske na tauhidi da ke wanzuwa tsakanin Kiristoci, amma ka bayyana matsayin da wannan nazari ya ɗauka da dalilinsa. Ka guji raina wasu al’adu.

Ka samar da sarari don kokwanto na gaske. Shirye-shiryen baftisma na iya fitowa da manyan tsoro — tsoro na magana a bainar jama’a, tsoro na martanin iyali, tsoro na ɗaukar alƙawari a bainar jama’a ga rayuwar da ba su tabbata za su iya kiyayewa ba. Waɗannan tsoro sun kasance na gaske. Ka tabbatar da cewa mahalarta sun san za su iya bayyana su ba tare da a yanke musu hukunci ko a matsa musu lamba ba.

Kada ka ɗauke wannan a matsayin jerin ayyuka da za a kammala. Manufar ba ita ce a kai wani ta cikin babi shida da sauri gwargwadon yiwuwa a sa shi cikin ruwa ba. Manufar ita ce mutumin da ya fahimci abin da yake yi, yana shirye na gaske don yi shi, kuma zai duba wa baftismarsa har tsawon rayuwarsa a matsayin lokacin bayyananiya da farin ciki.
intro3: Kuuje ɗee njiɗi nafaago ardiiɓe cokkal, paastoriiɓe, e ekkitinooɓe ɓe mballa yimɓe gooto gooto walla kawte kese nder janngirde ngam mbaadi batiismu nde. Ko waɗaa gooto-gooto bee jiɗɗo imanan keso walla ardotoo kawtal kese yimɓe mbaadi batiismu wootere, kuuje ɗee mballotoɗaa heɓugo nafa dow babi kala — ngam yimɓe ɗum ardotaa e ngam hoore maa.

Waɗu kuugal ngal a anndi kanko on aran. Ardiiɓe ɓuri mawnugo nder mbaadi batiismu ngoni ɓeen njooɗiɓe e ƴameeje ɗee dow ko’e maɓɓe ɓako ɓe mballa woɓɓe waɗugo bana non. Ko waɗaa batiisma ma on ko ɗuuɗi duuɓi ɓenni, yahnugo ɓaawo ko waɗiɗaa batiisma maa sabu mun — e ko jimaaɗo — wallataa ma waɗde jaranke ɓurɗo naftoraade.

Tiiɗnon ko ɓeen nder kawtal pati Kirista’en tano. Janngirde nde fuɗɗortoo haa ƴamaade tooŋŋe ko neɗɗo on Kirista. Taa nar ko huunde ko fuu jooɗiɗo ngam mbaadi batiismu imani goonga bee Iisa. Ɓeen feere ndaara. Ɓeen feere ngaɗan ko laato ngam naftorgo hoɓɓo familiijo. Ɗum waɗ bee jilli bee de’irre — amma taa yoɓ ɗum.

Ƴameede batiisma jarŋaare waɗata tawde.. Ɗum waɗ bee faabi. Yimɓe baatismaama ɗo ɓe njarii maa nannga ko ƴamminde ngon ngon waɗataa dow musiɓɓe maɓɓe walla aadaaji maɓɓe. Nannge jaayngol goonga nder tinnditaare e Kirista’en, kono yiiten haala mbaadi janngirde nde ñaawtiri to ngon sabu mun. Taa wonde nanndi cela aadaaje goɗɗe.

Tiggan nokkuure ngam ɗeewtere goonga. Mbaadi batiismu waɗa ummugo sunaare mawnde — sunaare wolwugo ɓe’en yimɓe, sunaare jaabaawol familiijo, sunaare tawtoreede baawɗe tele e nguurndam mo ɓuri mawnugo ɓe nanda ko mbaawi ɗaɓɓugo. Sunaare ɗee ngoni goonga. Tiiɗon ko nder kawtal ngal nanndii ko ɓe mbaawi wolwugo ɗum bee de’irre.

Taa naatnu ɗum gaa waɗde liite jannguɗe. Ɗam-ɗam wonaa waɗugo neɗɗo ɓaawo babi jeegom ɓurngo yaawi e naatnugo nder ndiyam. Ɗam-ɗam wonaa neɗɗo anndiniiɗo ko o waɗata, tiiɗɗo goonga ngam waɗugo, kadi ndaara batiisma mum dow nguurndam mum kala gaa wakati mawngo e weltaare.

keyPoints1.body:
This brief introductory chapter does two things: it frames the purpose of the study and it surfaces existing assumptions about baptism before the biblical content begins. Both matter.

The opening narrative (Ruth) is designed to be immediately relatable — particularly for people who were baptised as infants and are now wondering whether that was sufficient. Use it to open up the group before diving into content.

The two callouts on infant baptism and readiness set expectations honestly from the start: this study has a clear position, but it handles disagreement with respect.

keyPoints2.body:
Wannan gajeren babi na gabatarwa yana yin abubuwa biyu: yana tsara manufar nazarin kuma yana fitar da zato da ake da su game da baftisma kafin abun ciki na Littafi Mai Tsarki ya fara. Dukkansu suna da mahimmanci.

Labarin buɗewa (Rut) an tsara shi ne don a iya danganta shi kai tsaye — musamman ga mutanen da aka yi musu baftisma tun suna jarirai kuma yanzu suna mamaki ko hakan ya ishe su. Yi amfani da shi don buɗe rukunin kafin a fara abun ciki.

Bayanan biyu akan baftismar jariri da shirye-shirye suna saita tsammanin da gaskiya tun farko: wannan nazari yana da matsayi bayyananne, amma yana mu’amala da rashin jituwa da girmamawa.

keyPoints3.body:
Babi kese jaranke nde waɗa ko ɗiɗi: hollude ko janngirde nde ɗon waɗa e ummugo miiji gollaaɗi dow batiismu ɓako abbitaari Dewtaari fuɗɗoto. Fow njiɗi.

Tindol fuɗɗorde (Rut) njiɗi naftoraade tele — ko waɗaa ngam yimɓe baatismaama ɗo ɓe njarii e nde ndaarta ko dey haaj. Ɗum waɗ ngam uddugo kawtal ngal ɓako naatde nder abbitaari.

Binndi ɗiɗi dow batiisma jariŋŋaari e mbaadi ngam fuɗɗortoo ee nanngude miijoji goonga: janngirde nde woodii haala teeŋtunde, kono ɗum nannga jaayngol bee teddungal.

pastorals1.body:
This chapter asks participants to write down what they already think baptism is, and where that idea has come from. Be prepared for a wide range of answers — and for some answers that reveal significant misunderstanding (e.g. ’baptism is what makes you a Christian’, or ’I was done as a baby so I’m fine’).

The question about what the person hopes the study will answer is a useful pastoral barometer. Listen carefully — the answer often reveals what is really at stake for that individual: family pressure, theological confusion, genuine readiness, or something else entirely.

For group settings, the opening narrative about Ruth provides a gentle way in for people who feel awkward about the infant baptism question. You do not need to resolve the question in Chapter 0 — just acknowledge it and reassure participants that it will be addressed properly.

pastorals2.body:
Wannan babi yana tambayar mahalarta su rubuta abin da suke tsammanin baftisma shine, da kuma daga ina wannan ra’ayi ya fito. Ka shirya don keɓaɓɓun amsoshi iri-iri — da kuma wasu amsoshi da ke nuna babban rashin fahimta (misali ’baftisma shi ne wanda ke sa ka zama Kirista’, ko ’an yi mini tun ina jariri don haka na yi daidai’).

Tambayar game da abin da mutumin yana fata nazarin zai amsa taimako ne na kiwo. Saurara da hankali — amsoshi na yawan bayyana abin da ke da muhimmanci ga wannan mutum: matsin lamba na iyali, rikitar da tauhidi, shirye-shirye na gaske, ko wani abu dabam.

Ga yanayin rukuni, labarin buɗewa game da Rut yana ba da hanya mai laushi ga mutanen da ke jin kunyar tambayar baftismar jariri. Ba kwa buƙatar warware tambayar a Babi na 0 — kawai ka sani da ita kuma ka tabbatar wa mahalarta cewa za a magance ta yadda ya kamata.

pastorals3.body:
Babi nde ƴamatnoo nder kawtal ngal winndude ko ɓe nummata batiismu wonata, e ko miijo ɓuri umaade. Tiiɗon ngam jaabaaje toɗɗaaɗe — e jaabaaje ɓeen nanndude anndinowe (fii: ‘batiismu ko waɗata ma Kirista’, walla ’baatismaama ɗo njarii donngal’)

Ƴameede dow ko neɗɗo on nummata janngirde nde jaabaata wonaa kisal-hoore naftoranirde. Nanngo faabi — jaabaaje ɓen keɓata ko ɗon teeŋta ngam neɗɗo on: baɗdi familiijo, hecce tinnditaare, mbaadi goonga, walla huunde woɗɗunde.

Ngam kawte, tindol fuɗɗorde dow Rut hollude laawol seeɗa ngam yimɓe naannda bee ƴameede dow batiisma jariŋŋaari. Taa haaj warnitoyde ƴameede nder Babi 0 — nanngo ɗum e toɗɗin nder kawtal ngal ko ɗum jaabortoo goonga.

watch1.body: 👁️ Watch for participants who arrive with their minds already made up — either ’I definitely need to be baptised’ or ’I was done as a baby and this is just a formality.’ Both postures can prevent genuine engagement with the material.

watch2.header: Abubuwan da ya kamata a kula da su
watch2.body: 👁️ Ka lura da mahalarta waɗanda suka zo da tunaninsu a shirye — ko ’Lalle ina buƙatar a yi mini baftisma’ ko ’An yi mini tun ina jariri kuma wannan biki ne kawai.’ Dukkan matsayin biyu na iya hana shiga gaske da kayan aikin.

watch3.body: 👁️ Ndarna gite ma dow nder kawtal ngal njooɗiɓe bee miiji mum’en tiiɗuɗi — walla ’Tiiɗii ina haaj baatismaama’ walla ’Baatismaama ɗo njarii kadi ɗum wonaa fow.’ Mbaadi ɗiɗi ɗee ngona naftoraade heddude e abbitaari.

keyPoints1.body:
This chapter asks the most foundational question of the entire study: is the participant actually a Christian? Everything else depends on the answer. Do not rush past it.

The key distinction to draw out is between inherited faith and personal faith. Growing up in a Christian home, attending church, knowing Bible stories, being generally moral — none of these make a person a Christian. The chapter uses John’s story to press this gently but clearly.

The Acts 2 passage establishes the pattern of believe → repent → be baptised that runs through the whole New Testament. Help participants see that this is not one denomination’s interpretation — it is the shape of the gospel response from the very beginning of the church.

The Ethiopian official in Acts 8 is worth dwelling on — his urgency is instructive. Baptism is the normal, immediate, joyful first step of the Christian life, not a delayed ceremony.

keyPoints2.body:
Wannan babi yana tambayar tambayar da ta fi zama tushe ta dukkan nazarin: shin mahalarcin Kirista ne da gaske? Duk sauran abubuwa sun dogara da amsa. Kada ka yi hanzari a wucewa.

Babban bambanci da za a fitar shine tsakanin imani gado da imani na mutum ɗaya. Girma a cikin gidan Kirista, zuwa coci, sanin labarun Littafi Mai Tsarki, kasancewa mai kyau gabaɗaya — waɗannan ba sa sa mutumin ya zama Kirista. Wannan babi yana amfani da labarin Yahaya don matsa wannan da kyau amma a fili.

Wucin Ayyukan 2 yana kafa tsarin yi imani → tuba → a yi baftisma wanda ke gudana ta dukkan Sabon Alkawari. Taimaki mahalarta su ga cewa wannan ba fassarar ƙungiya ɗaya ba ne — ita ce sifar martanin bisharar daga farkon coci.

Jami’in Habasha a Ayyukan 8 yana cancanta a zauna a kai — gaggawarsa tana koyarwa. Baftisma shine mataki na farko na al’ada, na kai tsaye, da farin ciki na rayuwar Kirista.

keyPoints3.body:
Babi nde ƴamatnoo ƴameede ɓurnde teeŋtugo nder janngirde nde fow: ko nder kawtal ngal on Kirista goonga? Ko fow woɗɗungo ɗon feccitii dow jaabawol. Taa henyu.

Toɗɗinol teeŋtunol ngam ummugo wonaa feccol e tinndi imaan ngenndiyanke e imaan neɗɗanke. Jiɓineeɗo nder galle Kirista, naatde cokkal, anndugo tindol Dewtaari, wonde mo welɗo bana non — fow ngon waɗataa neɗɗo Kirista. Babi nde ɗon waɗugo tindol Yaaya ngam tawtoreede ɗum seeɗa kono teeŋtunde.

Tindol Kuuje 2 tiiɗinii mbaadi imanan → tuba → baatismaama nde mooɓtinoo nder Dewtaari Kesiri fow. Mball nder kawtal ngal nanngo ko ɗum wonaa fassira kawtal gooto — ko sifaa jaabaawol bishaare fuɗɗorde e cokkal fuɗɗorde.

Jami’iral Habashiya nder Kuuje 8 njiɗi njooɗeede dow mum — gaagaare mum ekkitinoore. Batiismu wonaa mataki fuɗɗorde buri naftoraade, tele, bee weltaare nder nguurndam Kirista.

pastorals1.body:
The question ’Are you a Christian?’ can land very differently depending on the participant’s background. For someone from a strongly Christian family, it may feel accusatory. For someone who came to faith recently and is still finding their footing, it may feel like an invitation to articulate something they have never put into words before. Calibrate your tone accordingly.

Be alert to participants who give fluent, correct theological answers but show little personal connection to what they are saying. Knowing the right words about repentance and faith is not the same as having repented and believed. Gentle follow-up questions (‘When did that happen for you?’, ‘What did that feel like?’) can help surface what is real.

The infant baptism callout in this chapter will need careful handling if any participants were baptised as infants. Acknowledge the tradition they come from with respect. Be clear about the position this study takes, but avoid making people feel that their family or church history has been invalidated. The goal is clarity, not confrontation.

If a participant is genuinely unsure whether they are a Christian, do not rush them toward baptism. Baptism preparation may become, for them, a journey toward faith — and that is a good thing. Keep the church leader informed and involved.

pastorals2.body:
Tambayar ’Shin Kirista ne?’ na iya sauka daban-daban dangane da asalin mahalarcin. Ga wanda ya fito daga dangi mai ƙarfin Kirista, na iya ji kamar zargi ne. Ga wanda ya zo ga imani kwanan nan kuma har yanzu yana neman ƙafafunsa, na iya ji kamar gayyata ne don bayyana wani abu da bai taɓa sanya cikin kalmomi ba. Ka daidaita sautin ka bisa haka.

Ka yi faɗakarwa ga mahalarta waɗanda ke ba da amsoshi na tauhidi masu gudana, daidai amma suna nuna ɗan haɗin kai tare da abin da suke faɗa. Sanin daidaitattun kalmomi game da tuba da imani ba ya kunshe da tuba da yarda. Tambayoyin bi da bi masu laushi (‘Yaushe hakan ya faru a gare ka?’, ‘Ya yaya hakan ya ji?’) na iya taimaka wajen fitar da abin da yake na gaske.

Bayanan baftismar jariri a wannan babi zai buƙaci kulawa ta musamman idan wasu mahalarta an yi musu baftisma tun suna jarirai. Ka yarda da al’adar da suka fito daga gare ta da girmamawa. Ka bayyana matsayin da wannan nazari ya ɗauka, amma ka guji sa mutane su ji cewa an soke tarihin iyalinsu ko cocin su. Manufar shine bayyananiya, ba ƙaramar husuma ba.

Idan mahalarcin bai tabbata ko Kirista ne ko ba, kada ka yi hanzari wajen turawa zuwa baftisma. Shirye-shiryen baftisma na iya zama, a gare su, tafiya zuwa ga imani — kuma wannan abu ne mai kyau. Ka ci gaba da sanar da shugaban coci kuma ya kasance ya shiga.

pastorals3.body:
Ƴameede ’Ko Kirista a?’ waɗataa tele dow asli neɗɗo nder kawtal ngal. Ngam neɗɗo ummiɗo nder galle Kirista tiiɗnde, maa nannga bana tudditaare. Ngam neɗɗo imanaaki ko ɗuuɗi ɗon laano kadi ndondi on, maa nannga bana weltagol ngam hollude ko ɓuri mawnugo ɓe mbaawaa hollude tele. Waɗ tinndi maa bana non.

Ndarna gite ma dow nder kawtal ngal njanngata jaabaaje tinnditaare woɗɗe, tooŋŋe kono nandataa bee haajugo tele e ko ɓe mbii. Anndugo kelmoje tooŋŋe dow tuba e imaan wonaa bana wonde tubiima e imanamaa. Ƴameeje tawtoreede seeɗa (‘To hono laati ngon ngam maa?’, ‘Ko weli ma?’) mballa ummugo ko goonga.

Binndi batiisma jariŋŋaari nder babi nde maa haaj naftoreede faabi ko nder kawtal ngal baatismaama ɗo njarii. Nannge aadaaji ummi ɓen bee teddungal. Yiit haala mbaadi janngirde nde ñaawtiri, kono taa waɗ yimɓe nannda ko tarihi familiijo maɓɓe walla cokkal mum’en batal. Ɗam-ɗam wonaa bitti, naa jaayngol tiiɗungol.

Ko neɗɗo nder kawtal ngal ɓuri mawnugo ko o Kirista ɗoo, taa henyu ɓe ngam batiismu. Mbaadi batiismu maa waɗde, ngam ɓe, laawol ngam imaan — kadi ɗum huunde welɗe. Tiiɗon ko ardiiɗo cokkal anndinii e ɓe nder ɗum.

watch1.body: 👁️ Watch for participants who can describe repentance accurately but show no sign of having personally repented — and for those who are so overwhelmed by the idea of public commitment that they are using doctrinal uncertainty as a cover for deeper fear.

watch2.header: Abubuwan da ya kamata a kula da su
watch2.body: 👁️ Ka lura da mahalarta waɗanda za su iya bayyana tuba da daidaito amma ba su nuna wata alama ta tuba da kansu ba — da kuma waɗanda ke da ɗimbin tsoro game da alƙawarin jama’a da ake amfani da rashin tabbas na akida a matsayin murfin tsoro mafi zurfi.

watch3.body: 👁️ Ndarna gite ma dow nder kawtal ngal njaɓɓi hollude tuba goonga kono nandataa e nanndugo ɓe tubiima — e ɓeen nandatnooɓe tiiɗi nder miijo tawtoreede tele waɗataɓe hecce tinnditaare bana mural ngam sunaare ɓurnde mawnugo.

keyPoints1.body:
This chapter establishes the three pictures of baptism: a bath (washing from sin), a burial (the death of the old life), and a birth (resurrection into new life). These are not three separate ideas — they interlock and reinforce one another. Make sure participants can articulate all three before moving on.

The foundational theological point is that baptism does not make you a Christian — it declares what has already happened. This matters enormously in a Nigerian context where there can be a cultural pull toward seeing ceremonies and rituals as having power in themselves. The wedding ring illustration is helpful here.

Romans 6:1–14 is theologically dense. Do not feel you need to work through every verse in detail in a group setting. Focus on the core movement: united with Christ in death → united with Christ in resurrection → therefore live differently. The practical application in verses 12–13 is the payoff.

The question of full immersion vs. sprinkling should be handled clearly but without creating unnecessary controversy. The Greek word, the pictures of burial and resurrection, and the practice of the early church all point toward immersion. State this clearly and move on.

keyPoints2.body:
Wannan babi yana kafa hotuna uku na baftisma: wanka (tsarkake daga zunubi), binne (mutuwar rayuwar tsoho), da haihuwa (tashin matattu zuwa sabuwar rayuwa). Waɗannan ba ra’ayoyi uku daban-daban ba ne — suna haɗuwa da ƙarfafa juna. Ka tabbatar da cewa mahalarta za su iya bayyana dukkan uku kafin su ci gaba.

Babban mahimmin mahimmin tauhidi shine cewa baftisma bai sa ka zama Kirista ba — yana bayyana abin da ya riga ya faru

. Wannan yana da muhimmanci sosai a yanayin Najeriya inda yana iya zama jarabar al’adu na ganin bikin da al’adun gargajiya a matsayin suna da iko a cikin kansu. Misalin zobe na aure yana taimaka a nan.

Romawa 6:1–14 mai yawan tauhidi ne. Kada ka ji kamar kana buƙatar yin nazarin kowane aya a tsari a yanayin rukuni. Ka mai da hankali kan motsin tsakiya: haɗuwa da Kiristi a mutuwa → haɗuwa da Kiristi a tashin matattu → don haka rayuwa daban. Amfanin aikace-aikace a aya 12–13 shine sakamako.

Tambayar nutsarwa cikakke da zubar da ruwa ya kamata a yi mu’amala da ita a fili amma ba tare da haifar da rikici marar buƙata ba. Kalmar Girkanci, hotuna na binne da tashin matattu, da al’adar cocin farko duk suna nuna ga nutsarwa. Ka bayyana wannan a fili kuma ka ci gaba.

keyPoints3.body:
Babi nde tiiɗinii nanndinaje tati ngam batiismu: wuddaare (ɓalnude dow juumre), laabude (mayde nguurndam gaa’o), e jiɓineede (ummugo nguurndam kesum). Ɗon wonaa miijo tati toɗɗaaɗi — ɗon naatni e ɓurtini goɗɗum. Tiiɗon ko nder kawtal ngal mbaawi hollude tati ɗon ɓako yahugo dow.

Tinnditaare tinnditande teeŋtunde nden wonaa batiismu waɗataa ma Kirista — ɗum hollitiima ko laatinooma. Ɗum teeŋti ko o’ore nder Nijeriya ɓe nder miiji aadaaji ngona waɗde baawɗe nder ko’e maɓɓe. Nanndinande zobe nikka ɗon naftorante.

Roomaŋkoobe 6:1–14 tiiɗi nder tinnditaare. Taa nannga ko haaj waɗugo dow aaya kala faabi nder kawtal. Tiiɗon dow fitirgol teeŋtungol: naatteede bee Kirista nder mayde → naatteede bee Kirista nder ummugo → don waɗu nguurndam kesum. Golle-gollanaango nder aayaaji 12–13 wonaa nafaare.

Ƴameede nutsaraare fow kaa e bullaango maa haaj waɗde faabi kono de’irre taa umma jaayngol maayangol. Kelmol Girik ngol, nanndinaje laabude e ummugo, e aadaaji cokkal fuɗɗorde fow mbijii nutsaraare. Hol ɗum teeŋtude e yahu dow.

pastorals1.body:
The wedding ring illustration works well in most Nigerian contexts, but be sensitive to participants who may have complicated feelings about marriage. You can substitute another illustration if needed — a passport, an ID card, a certificate — any document that declares a status that already exists rather than creating it.

The question ’What was your old self like?’ can be a very vulnerable moment for participants. Some will have come from backgrounds involving significant sin or pain. Others may feel their story is not dramatic enough to be worth telling. Both responses miss the point. The ’old self’ is simply the self that lived without Christ at the centre — that is true for everyone, whatever the specifics.

Romans 6 makes the strong claim that the Christian’s old self has been ’crucified with Christ.’ Some participants may struggle with this — they still feel the pull of old habits, old patterns, old ways of thinking. Help them understand that the indicative (‘you have died to sin’) comes before the imperative (‘count yourselves dead to sin’). The reality precedes the application. Sin’s power is broken even when its pull is still felt.

The ’What if I mess up afterwards?’ question is likely to surface here even if it appears formally in later chapters. Address it honestly: sin after baptism is expected, not disqualifying. What matters is what you do with it.

pastorals2.body:
Misalin zobe na aure yana aiki sosai a mafi yawan yanayin Najeriya, amma ka kasance mai kulawa ga mahalarta waɗanda na iya samun ji rikitarwa game da aure. Za ka iya maye gurbin wani misali idan ya cancanta — fasfo, katin shaida, takardar shaidar — kowane takarda da ke bayyana matsayin da ya riga wanzu maimakon ƙirƙirar shi.

Tambayar ’Yaya tsohon kanka ya kasance?’ na iya zama lokaci mai rauni sosai ga mahalarta. Wasu za su fito daga baya da ya haɗa da zunubi mai girma ko zafi. Wasu na iya ji cewa labarin su bai yi ban mamaki ishe ya cancanta a faɗa ba. Martanin biyu suna rasa manufa. ’Tsohon kai’ kawai shine kai da ya rayu ba tare da Kiristi a tsakiya ba — hakan gaskiya ne ga kowa, duk abin da ke ciki.

Romawa 6 yana yin iƙirari mai ƙarfi cewa tsohon kansa na Kirista ya ’rataye akan gicciye tare da Kiristi.’ Wasu mahalarta na iya yin gwagwarmaya da wannan — har yanzu suna jin jarabar tsoffin al’adu, tsoffin tsare-tsare, tsoffin hanyoyin tunani. Taimake su su fahimci cewa ma’anar ainihi (‘kun mutu ga zunubi’) ya zo kafin umarni (‘ku ɗauka kanku sun mutu ga zunubi’). Hakikar tana riga amfanin. Ana karya ikon zunubi ko da har yanzu ana ji jaraba.

Tambayar ’Yaya idan na lalace bayan haka?’ mai yiwuwa za ta fito nan ma ko da ta bayyana a hukumance a babi masu zuwa. Ka magance ta da gaskiya: zunubi bayan baftisma ana tsammanin sa, ba yanke cancantar ba. Abin da ke da mahimmanci shine abin da ka yi da shi.

pastorals3.body:
Nanndinande zobe nikka naftorante ko o’ore nder Nijeriya, kono tiiɗon dow nder kawtal ngal nandatnooɓe miiji ɓurɗi ko dow nikka. Mbaawɗaa heɓde nanndinande woɗɗunde ko haaj — paasipoor, kaarta, saakitorde — takke kala hollitiinde mbaadi gollaaɗo naa waɗɗugo mum.

Ƴameede ’Ko nder nawtoram gaa’am weli ma?’ waɗata e wakati naannde ko o’ore ngam nder kawtal ngal. Ɓeen feere njooɗata bee tarihi juumre mawnde walla njaari. Woɓɓe maa nanda ko tindol maɓɓe wonaa teeŋtude yo ɓe mbaawi hollude. Jaabaaje fow ɗon naatata. ’Hoore gaa’o’ ko tan hoore nguurndinoore bee Kirista nder nokkuure — ɗum goonga ngam fow, ko fuu waɗaa.

Roomaŋkoobe 6 holtini tiiɗnugol ko hoore gaa’o Kirista ’laabtinooma bee Kirista.’ Nder kawtal ngal feere ngona e yiylaade ɗum — ɓe nannda tan jaɓɓugol aadaaji gaa’i, sifaaji gaa’i, miijoji gaa’i. Mball ɓe nanngo ko mbaadi goonga (‘muuɗon dow juumre’) aran ɓaawo umarnaare (‘miijton ko muuɗon dow juumre’). Goonga fuɗɗortoo golle. Baawɗe juumre keɓata ko ɓe nannda tan jaɓɓugol.

Ƴameede ’Ko hono waɗoto ko yonaago batiismu?’ maa umma ɗo ko waɗaa nder babi dow. Jaabu tooŋŋe: juumre ɓaawo batiismu tawdetee, naa warte cancanal. Ko teeŋtata wonaa ko waɗaa bee mum.

watch1.body: 👁️ Watch for participants who intellectually grasp the three pictures but have no personal connection to them — who can explain ’buried with Christ’ but cannot say what their old self actually was, or what is now different. The pictures should illuminate a real story, not just illustrate a doctrine.

watch2.header: Abubuwan da ya kamata a kula da su
watch2.body: 👁️ Ka lura da mahalarta waɗanda suke fahimtar hotuna uku ta hankali amma ba su da haɗin kai na mutum tare da su — waɗanda za su iya bayyana ’an binne tare da Kiristi’ amma ba za su iya faɗa ko tsohon kansunsu da gaske ya kasance ba, ko abin da yanzu ya bambanta. Hotuna ya kamata su haskaka labari na gaske, ba kawai misalin koyarwa ba.

watch3.body: 👁️ Ndarna gite ma dow nder kawtal ngal nanndinii nanndinaje tati kono nandataa bee haajugo tele dow ɓe — nannduɓe mbaawi hollude ’laabtinooma bee Kirista’ kono ngona waawi wolwugo ko hoore maɓɓe gaa’o waɗii, walla ko rewitii. Nanndinaje ɗon maa hollude tindol goonga, naa tan waɗde misali tinnditaare.

keyPoints1.body:
This chapter introduces the dimension of baptism that is most often overlooked: belonging. Baptism is not only a personal declaration — it is an entry into a community. The two passages (Matthew 28 and Ephesians 4) together build the case that this community is both global and local.

The Great Commission (Matthew 28:18–20) establishes that baptism is inseparable from disciple-making. It is not a standalone ceremony — it is the entry point of a discipled life. Making disciples involves baptising and teaching together. One without the other is incomplete.

Ephesians 4’s ’one baptism’ is theologically significant: Paul places baptism alongside ’one Lord, one faith, one God’ as a foundation of Christian unity. This elevates baptism beyond a personal milestone to a shared mark of the global body of Christ.

The local church emphasis at the end of the chapter is important and practical. Help participants identify specifically what their local church involvement looks like — not just ’I attend on Sundays’ but genuine belonging, serving, and being known.

keyPoints2.body:
Wannan babi yana gabatar da fanni na baftisma wanda aka fi yin watsi da shi: mallakar. Baftisma ba bayyanar mutum ɗaya kawai ba ne — shine shigar da al’umma. Wucegan biyu (Matta 28 da Afisawa 4) tare suna gina hujjar cewa wannan al’umma tana cikin duniya baki ɗaya da gida.

Babban Umarni (Matta 28:18–20) yana kafa cewa baftisma ba za a iya raba shi da samar da almajiri ba. Ba bikin da yake tsaye shi kaɗai ba — shine ƙofar rayuwar almajiri. Yin almajiri ya haɗa da baftisma da koyarwa tare. Ɗaya ba tare da ɗayan ba ba cikakke ba ne.

’Baftisma ɗaya’ na Afisawa 4 yana da muhimmin tauhidi: Bulus yana sanya baftisma tare da ’Ubangiji ɗaya, imani ɗaya, Allah ɗaya’ a matsayin tushen haɗin kai na Kirista. Wannan yana ɗaukaka baftisma sama da alamar mutum ɗaya zuwa alama ta baitulmalin jiki na duniya na Kiristi.

Jaddadawar cocin gida a ƙarshen babi ya muhimmanci kuma yana da amfani. Taimaki mahalarta su gano musamman abin da haɗin kai na cocin gida na su ya yi kama — ba kawai ’Ina halartar Lahadi’ amma mallakar gaske, hidima, da a san su.

keyPoints3.body:
Babi nde naatna ɗertirgel batiismu nde ɓuri mawnugo yiylaade: tawtoreede. Batiismu wonaa tan hollugo neɗɗanke — ɗum naatneede kawtal. Tindol ɗiɗol (Mattaay 28 e Efesiŋkoobe 4) taaroo mbinndoo ko kawtal nde wonaa dowri e lesdi e gese.

Umarnaare Mawnde (Mattaay 28:18–20) tiiɗinii ko batiismu feccitaake e waɗugo almajiri. Ɗum wonaa biɗto ɗon yontii — ɗum naatneede nguurndam almajiri. Waɗugo almajiri naatna baatismaango e ekkinde taaroo. Goɗɗo bee goɗɗo wonaa fow.

’Batiismu gooto’ nder Efesiŋkoobe 4 tiiɗi nder tinnditaare: Buulus naatna batiismu bee ’Joomiraaɗo gooto, imaan gooto, Alla gooto’ bana asli teewtere Kirista’en. Ɗum ɓurtina batiismu dow taaroo e coŋngol baitulmaali yimɓe Kiristi fow.

Teeŋtinde cokkal lesdi nder ɓaawo babi nde teeŋti e naftorante. Mball nder kawtal ngal ɓe finditoo teeŋtude ko tawtoreede cokkal lesdi maɓɓe weli — naa tan ’nde naatdata Alhamiisa’ kono tawtoreede goonga, gollorgo, e anndinaneede.

pastorals1.body:
Rachel’s story — the surprise of discovering that baptism was about belonging as much as declaring — resonates particularly well in Nigerian contexts where community and family solidarity are deeply valued. Use it to open up the question of who participants see themselves as belonging to, and what they are being invited into.

The question about local church involvement can be uncomfortable for people who attend services without being genuinely embedded in a church community. Do not let participants get away with vague answers here. Press gently but specifically: ‘Who in your church knows you well? Where are you serving? Who would notice if you stopped coming?’

The two bonus callouts in this chapter (public baptism and communion) are important but should not dominate discussion. They are best handled briefly in a group setting and more fully in one-to-one conversation if a specific participant needs to work through them.

By the end of this chapter, participants should be moving toward a clear decision. If someone is still very undecided, it is worth pausing here — before the practical planning of Chapter 4 — to understand what is really holding them back.

pastorals2.body:
Labarin Rachel — mamaki na gano cewa baftisma ta shafi mallakar kamar bayyanar — yana yin sauti sosai a yanayin Najeriya inda ake kimanta haɗin kai na al’umma da iyali sosai. Yi amfani da shi don buɗe tambayar wane ne mahalarta ke ganin kansu a matsayin mallakar, da kuma abin da ake gayyatarsu a ciki.

Tambayar haɗin kai na cocin gida na iya zama maras daɗi ga mutanen da ke halarta hidimomi ba tare da an binne su sosai a al’ummar coci ba. Kada ka bari mahalarta su sami amshi mara tabbas a nan. Ka matsa da laushi amma musamman: ‘Wane ne a cikin cocin ka ya san ka sosai? Ina kake hidima? Wane ne zai lura idan ka daina zuwa?’

Bayanan biya na bonus a wannan babi (baftisma a bainar jama’a da tarayyar kari) suna da muhimmanci amma bai kamata su mamaye tattaunawa ba. A fi kyau a yi mu’amala da su a taƙaice a yanayin rukuni kuma cikakke a tattaunawa ɗaya-da-ɗaya idan wani mahalarcin musamman yana buƙatar yin nazarinsu.

A ƙarshen wannan babi, mahalarta ya kamata su matsa zuwa ga shawarar bayyanana. Idan wani har yanzu bai yanke shawara sosai ba, ya cancanta a tsaya nan — kafin shirin aikace-aikace na Babi na 4 — don fahimtar abin da ke riƙe shi da gaske.

pastorals3.body:
Tindol Rachel — weltaare nder heɓugo ko batiismu wonaa tawtoreede bana hollugo — naftorante ko o’ore nder Nijeriya to kawtal e haɗde familiijo njiɗi ko o’ore. Ɗum waɗ ngam uddugo ƴameede ko nder kawtal ngal nandii ko ɓe tawtoroo e ko ɓe njaadetee naatde.

Ƴameede dow tawtoreede cokkal lesdi waɗata e naannde ngam yimɓe naatdiiɓe ee nder kawtal kono ngon tawtoraaɓe goonga. Taa waɗ nder kawtal ngal ɓe heɓa jaabaaje miijaaje. Teeŋtinon seeɗa kono faabi: ‘Moy nder cokkal maa anndinii ma faabi? To a golloraa? Moy maa yiya ko a hawtii?’

Binndi ɗiɗi goɗɗi nder babi nde (batiismu tele e haɗoowo tarayyar kari) teeŋti kono taa mbiiɗen dow wakkati ɗuuɗo. Ɓuri naftoraade jaabaade ɗum seeɗa nder kawtal e teeŋtunde nder haalde gooto-gooto ko neɗɗo feere nder kawtal ngal haaj waɗde ɗum.

Ɓaawo babi nde, nder kawtal ngal maa fiɗtoyde ngam ñaawtaari teeŋtunde. Ko neɗɗo ɗon woɗɗi ko ñaawtaari, njiɗi toɗɗande ɗo — ɓako golle-gollanaango nder Babi 4 — ngam anndugo ko teeŋtata ɓe.

watch1.body: 👁️ Watch for participants who grasp the global dimension of belonging (‘I’m part of the worldwide church’) but resist the local dimension (‘I don’t really need to be committed to a specific congregation’). The global cannot be experienced without the local.

watch2.header: Abubuwan da ya kamata a kula da su
watch2.body: 👁️ Ka lura da mahalarta waɗanda suke fahimtar fanni na duniya na mallakar (‘Ina cikin cocin duniya’) amma suna ƙin fanni na gida (‘Ba lalle ne in kasance da alƙawari ga tarayya ta musamman’). Ba za a iya samun duniyar ba tare da gida ba.

watch3.body: 👁️ Ndarna gite ma dow nder kawtal ngal nanndinii ɗertirgel dowri tawtoreede (‘Minen nder cokkal fow nder adunaaru’) kono mbaaɗa ɗertirgel lesdi (‘Ina haaj tawtoreede cokkal teeŋtungol’). Dowri maa yiylaade bee lesdi.

keyPoints1.body:
This is the most practical chapter of the study. Its primary purpose is to move the participant from decision to preparation. The key leadership task here is helping people fix a real date, with real plans, involving real people.

The section on testimony preparation is the most significant content in the chapter. A well-prepared, honest, Jesus-centred testimony is one of the most powerful things a baptism service can offer the watching congregation and any guests who are not yet Christians. Invest significant time here.

The three-part testimony shape (before / how / since) is deliberately simple. Some participants may feel their story is too ordinary or too messy to be worth telling. Push back on this directly: no story of someone being brought from death to life through Jesus is ordinary or too messy.

The remaining bonus questions in this chapter (not good enough, mess it up, parents’ opposition, mockery) are the most pastorally sensitive of the study. Handle them with care, and be ready to follow up one-to-one if any of them land hard with a particular participant.

keyPoints2.body:
Wannan shine mafi amfanin babi na nazarin. Babban manufarsa ita ce matsar da mahalarcin daga yanke shawara zuwa shirye-shirye. Babban aikin jagoranci a nan yana taimaka wa mutane su gyara kwanan wata na gaske, tare da shirye-shiryen gaske, da haɗin mutane na gaske.

Sashin shirye-shiryen shaida shine mafi muhimmancin abun ciki a cikin babi. Shaida mai shirye-shirye, gaskiya, mai mai da hankali ga Yesu ita ce ɗaya daga cikin mafi ƙarfin abubuwan da hidimar baftisma za ta iya bayarwa ga taron masu sauraro da kowane baƙi waɗanda ba Kiristoci ba tukuna. Ka zuba lokaci mai mahimmanci nan.

Sifar shaida mai sassa uku (kafin / yadda / tun da) ta sauƙi a gangan. Wasu mahalarta na iya ji cewa labarin su na yau da kullun ko rikitarwa sosai ya cancanta a faɗa. Ka ƙin wannan kai tsaye: babu labari na wani da aka kawo daga mutuwa zuwa ga rayuwa ta wurin Yesu wanda yake na yau da kullun ko mai rikitarwa sosai.

Tambayoyin bonus da suka rage a wannan babi (ba isasshen nagarta, lalace, ƙin iyaye, izgili) sune mafi kulawa na kiwo na nazarin. Yi mu’amala da su da kulawa, kuma ka kasance shirye don bin da ɗaya-da-ɗaya idan wani daga cikinsu ya tsaya da wuya tare da wani mahalarcin musamman.

keyPoints3.body:
Ɗam-ɗam babi ɓurngo golle-gollanaango nder janngirde nde. Ko ɗam-ɗam njiɗi ko ƴamdude neɗɗo nder kawtal ngal dow ñaawtaari ngam mbaadi. Kuugal ardiiɗo teeŋtungol ɗo wonaa mball yimɓe tiiɗinde raawangu goonga, bee golle-gollanaango goonga, tawndude yimɓe goonga.

Ɗertirgel mbaadi shahaada nde teeŋtunde nder abbitaari nder babi nde. Shahaada mbadinoonde, goonga, nanndiinde Iisa nde wonaa huunde ɓurnde baawɗe nde hidimaa batiismu waɗda nder kawtal taawtoreewol e baraaɓe ko njooɗaaki tano Kirista. Naatnu wakati teeŋtungol ɗo.

Sifaa shahaada tati (ɓaawo / no / tuma) seeɗi ko ɗum-ɗum. Nder kawtal ngal feere maa nanda ko tindol mum’en yimirtaaki walla ngonaa teeŋtude yo ɓe mbaawi hollude. Taa nanndu ɗum tele: tindol fuu neɗɗo naatnaaɗo dow mayde ngam nguurndam bee Iisa wonaa yimirtaaki walla teeŋtaaki.

Ƴameeje bonus gollaaɗe nder babi nde (cancanal wonaa teeŋtungol, yonaago, baɗdi aynaaɓe, ɓernaaɓe) ngoni ɓurɗe heɓde ngam kisal nder janngirde nde. Waɗ bee faabi, e tiiɗon ngam tawtoreede gooto-gooto ko nder kawtal ngal feere tawde ɗum tiiɗi.

pastorals1.body:
Adam’s quiet, undramatic ’yes’ is deliberately chosen as the opening narrative for this chapter. In a Nigerian church context there can sometimes be pressure to have a dramatic conversion story or a highly emotional response. Adam’s story normalises the quiet, considered decision — which is just as valid.

When helping participants prepare their testimony, watch for two opposite errors. The first is a testimony that is entirely about the participant — their feelings, their journey, their growth — with Jesus appearing only as a supporting character. The second is a testimony that is so theologically dense it loses the personal story entirely. Help participants find the balance: personal enough to be moving, Jesus-centred enough to be gospel.

The practical planning questions (date, venue, outfit, songs) can feel trivial alongside the theological content of earlier chapters. Resist that impression. These details matter because they make the baptism real, help participants invite people, and reduce anxiety on the day. A participant who has thought through these details is a participant who is genuinely committed to going through with it.

Be particularly attentive to participants who have significant family opposition. This is common in Nigerian contexts — particularly where a person is the first Christian in their family, or where they are being baptised as a believer having previously been identified with infant baptism in a different tradition. These situations need sensitive, wise, one-to-one pastoral care.

pastorals2.body:
An zabi ’ee’ mai shuru, mai sauƙi na Adam a gangan a matsayin labarin buɗewa na wannan babi. A yanayin cocin Najeriya a wani lokaci na iya zama matsi na samun labarin tuba mai ban mamaki ko martanin da ke da ɗimbin motsin rai. Labarin Adam yana sa yanke shawara mai shuru, mai tunani ya zama na yau da kullun — wanda ya fi inganci daidai.

Lokacin taimaka wa mahalarta su shirya shaida su, ka kula da kurakuran biyu masu adawa. Na farko shine shaida wacce dukka ta shafi mahalarcin — ji su, tafiya su, girma su — tare da Yesu yana bayyana kawai a matsayin hali mai tallafi. Na biyu shine shaida mai yawan tauhidi haka ta rasa labarin mutum gaba ɗaya. Taimaki mahalarta su sami daidaito: na mutum ishe don motsa, mai mai da hankali ga Yesu ishe don zama bisharar.

Tambayoyin shirye-shirye na aikace-aikace (ranar, wurin, tufafi, waƙoƙi) na iya ji kamar suna da ƙarancin muhimmanci tare da abun ciki na tauhidi na babi na farko. Ka ƙi wannan ra’ayi. Waɗannan bayanai suna da mahimmanci saboda suna sa baftisma ta kasance ta gaske, suna taimaka wa mahalarta su gayyaci mutane, kuma suna rage damuwa a ranar. Mahalarcin da ya yi tunani game da waɗannan bayanai mahalarcin ne wanda ke da alƙawari na gaske don ci gaba da shi.

Ka kasance mai kulawa musamman ga mahalarta waɗanda ke da adawar iyali mai muhimmanci. Wannan na yau da kullun ne a yanayin Najeriya — musamman inda mutumin shine Kirista na farko a cikin danginsu, ko inda ake yi musu baftisma a matsayin mai imani bayan an gano su da baftismar jariri a al’ada dabam. Waɗannan yanayi suna buƙatar kulawa mai hankali, masu hikima, na kiwo ɗaya-da-ɗaya.

pastorals3.body:
’Iwo’ seeɗi, de’irde ngam Adam suɓtinaama ɗum-ɗum bana tindol fuɗɗorde ngam babi nde. Nder yonki cokkal Nijeriya nder wakkati feere waɗata baɗdi tindol mayde ban’de walla jaabaawol bee weltaare mawnde. Tindol Adam waɗata ñaawtaari seeɗi, miijitaari yimirte — nde ɓuri naftoraade tele dawi.

Tuma a mballataa nder kawtal ngal ɓe mbaadon shahaada maɓɓe, ndarna gite ma dow juumre ɗiɗi ɗon adda. Fuɗɗannde wonaa shahaada fow dow neɗɗo nder kawtal ngal — nannaabe, laawol maɓɓe, ummugo maɓɓe — bee Iisa nanndiima tan bana haalande. Ɗimmande wonaa shahaada nde tiiɗi ko o’ore tinnditaare nde yowtii tindol neɗɗanke fow. Mball nder kawtal ngal heɓde feccol: neɗɗanke yo waɗa naftorante, nanndiinde Iisa yo waɗa bishaare.

Ƴameeje golle-gollanaango (raawangu, nokkuure, dooki, jimɗe) maa nanda ko hinnditaake bee abbitaari tinnditaare babi ɗimmbii. Taa nanndu miijo ngon. Binndaɗe ɗee teeŋti sabu ɗon waɗa batiismu goonga, mball nder kawtal ngal ɓe mbilloo yimɓe, e heddude sunaare nder raawangu. Neɗɗo miijinooɗo dow binndaɗe ɗee wonaa neɗɗo tiiɗninooɗo goonga ngam waɗde ɗum.

Tiiɗon teeŋtude dow nder kawtal ngal nandatnooɓe baɗdi familiijo mawnde. Ɗum yimirtaake nder Nijeriya — ko waɗaa ngam neɗɗo fuɗɗorde Kirista nder familiijo mum, walla to baatismaama bana jiɗɗo imanan baaɓe tawi ɓe gollodaanoo batiisma jariŋŋaari nder aadaaji woɗɗi. Ciiɗe ɗee njiɗi kisal seeɗi, anndindiniiɗo, gooto-gooto.

watch1.body: 👁️ Watch for participants whose testimony draft is essentially a description of church attendance and good behaviour rather than a personal encounter with the gospel. Gently but clearly redirect: ‘That tells me what you did — help me understand what Jesus did.’

watch2.header: Abubuwan da ya kamata a kula da su
watch2.body: 👁️ Ka lura da mahalarta waɗanda rubutun shaida su shine a asashe bayanin halarta coci da kyakkyawan hali maimakon haduwar mutum da bisharar. Ka jagoranci da laushi amma a fili: ‘Wannan yana gaya mani abin da ka yi — taimake ni fahimtar abin da Yesu ya yi.’

watch3.body: 👁️ Ndarna gite ma dow nder kawtal ngal winndol shahaada maɓɓe ko teeŋtude hollirde naatde cokkal e wonde welɗo naa haɗde tele e bishaare. Ƴamd tiiɗnude kono faabi: ‘Ɗun hollata ko a waɗii — mball mi anndugo ko Iisa waɗii.’

keyPoints1.body:
This final chapter serves two very different purposes and should be approached accordingly. Part 1 (the baptism planning notes) is essentially a practical preparation tool — its value lies in participants actually completing it, not just reading it. Part 2 (the Q&A) is a reference section that participants may return to repeatedly, both before and after their baptism.

The testimony planning fields in Part 1 are the most important content in the chapter. A participant who has written out all four sections (before / how / since / without him) and shown them to their church leader before the day is far better prepared than one who plans to speak off the cuff. Make completing this a non-negotiable part of preparation.

Part 2 covers twelve questions. In a group setting, do not attempt to work through all twelve — they are a reference tool, not a curriculum. Identify which one or two questions are most relevant or pressing for your particular group or individual, and focus the discussion there. The rest can be read privately.

The question on the Holy Spirit (Q1) deserves particular attention in Nigerian church contexts where charismatic theology is prevalent. Be clear and confident: the Holy Spirit is received at the moment of faith, not in a subsequent experience. This is not a denial of gifts or experience — it is a clarification of when the Spirit comes to indwell the believer.

keyPoints2.body:
Wannan babi na ƙarshe yana hidima ga manufofi biyu mabambanta sosai kuma ya kamata a gabatar da shi bisa haka. Kashi na 1 (bayanan shirye-shiryen baftisma) a asashe kayan aiki ne na shirye-shirye na aikace-aikace — darajarsa tana cikin mahalarta da ke kammala shi da gaske, ba kawai karanta shi ba. Kashi na 2 (amsa tambayoyi) bangare ne na tunani wanda mahalarta za su iya komawa a kai lokuta da yawa, kafin da kuma bayan baftisma su.

Filayen shirye-shiryen shaida a Kashi na 1 sune mafi muhimmancin abun ciki a cikin babi. Mahalarcin da ya rubuta sassa huɗu duka (kafin / yadda / tun da / ba tare da shi ba) kuma ya nuna su ga shugabansa na coci kafin ranar an fi shirye-shirye sosai fiye da wanda ya shirya yin magana ba tare da shirye-shirye ba. Ka sa kammalar wannan wani bangare na shirye-shirye na ba za a iya musantawa ba.

Kashi na 2 ya rufe tambayoyi goma sha biyu. A yanayin rukuni, kada ka yi ƙoƙari zuwa ga kowane sha biyu — su kayan tunani ne, ba manhaja ba. Ka gano wace tambaya ɗaya ko biyu sune mafi dacewa ko matsin lamba ga rukunin ku musamman ko mutum ɗaya, kuma ka mai da hankali kan tattaunawa a nan. Sauran za a iya karanta su a sirri.

Tambayar game da Ruhun Sama (Q1) ta cancanta kulawa ta musamman a yanayin cocin Najeriya inda tauhidi mai farin jini ya zama ruwan dare. Ka kasance a fili kuma ka kasance da tabbas: ana karɓar Ruhun Sama a lokacin imani, ba a cikin kwarewar baya ba. Wannan ba shi ne musun kyaututtuka ko kwarewa ba — shine bayyanawa lokacin da Ruhu ya zo zauna a cikin mai imani.

keyPoints3.body:
Babi ɓaawo ngon gollorto golle ɗiɗi toɗɗaaɗe ko o’ore kadi maa waɗde bana non. Ɗertirgel 1 (binndi golle-gollanaango batiismu) wonaa kiiɓororgal golle-gollanaango teeŋtungol — nafaa mum laata nder kawtal ngal kammindol ɗum goonga, naa tan janngol mum. Ɗertirgel 2 (jaabaaje ƴameeje) wonaa ɗertirgel tunginol nder kawtal ngal ngal maa tawo laata e nder waktuuji ɗuuɗi, ɓaawo e ɓaawo batiismu maɓɓe.

Binndi mbaadi shahaada nder Ɗertirgel 1 ngoni abbitaari ɓurnde teeŋtugo nder babi nde. Neɗɗo nder kawtal ngal winndunooɗo ɗertirgel nay fow (ɓaawo / no / tuma / bee de’irre mum) e holitnooɗo ɓe ngam ardiiɗo mum cokkal ɓako raawangu mbaadi ɓurnde teeŋtude faa naa waɗnooɗo ngam wolwugo bee de’irre. Waɗ kammude ɗum huunde maa haɗde e mbaadi.

Ɗertirgel 2 moɓtii ƴameeje sappo e ɗiɗi. Nder kawtal, taa feewnu ngam fow sappo e ɗiɗi — ngoni kiiɓororgal tunginol, naa manhaja. Finditoo ƴameede gooto walla ɗiɗi ɓurɗe naftoraade walla teeŋtugo ngam kawtal maa teeŋtungol walla neɗɗo teeŋtungol, e tiiɗon dow haalde ɗo. Goɗɗe mbaawi janngaade nder sirru.

Ƴameede dow Ruuhu Seniiɗo (Y1) haaj kulol teeŋtungol nder yonki cokkal Nijeriya to tinnditaare karismatik wondi. Wonde teeŋtungol e tiiɗningol: Ruuhu Seniiɗo heɓetee nder waktu imaan, naa nder kewkewe ɓaawo. Ɗum wonaa dartinde jaalataale walla kewkewe — ɗum tinndinirde to Ruuhu naata nder jiɗɗo imanan.

pastorals1.body:
Part 1 works best as a one-to-one exercise rather than a group activity. The testimony sections in particular are personal enough that participants benefit from privacy when writing — and from a one-to-one conversation with their church leader when reviewing what they have written. If you are working in a group, consider giving participants time to work on Part 1 individually before the session, and then using the group time to review and encourage rather than to write from scratch.

For the Q&A in Part 2, the pastorally most sensitive questions are likely to be Q2 (infant baptism), Q6 (messing up after baptism), Q10 (parental opposition), and Q11 (whether there is a good reason not to be baptised). Be ready to give extended time to any of these if a participant raises them personally — the written answers are a starting point, not a complete pastoral response.

The Holy Spirit question (Q1) may generate strong reactions in contexts where a ’second blessing’ theology is taught or assumed. Be clear and confident in your response, but avoid being dismissive of people’s genuine spiritual experiences. The point is not that they have not experienced the Spirit — it is that they received him at the moment of faith, even if their awareness of his work has grown over time.

The final reflection questions at the end of Part 2 — particularly the invitation to write a personal prayer — are significant. Do not rush past them. A participant who can write an honest prayer about their baptism has done something real. Take time to acknowledge it.

After completing this chapter, the next step is practical: fixing the date and beginning the visible preparations. Make sure this conversation happens clearly before the participant leaves the session.

pastorals2.body:
Kashi na 1 yana aiki mafi kyau a matsayin aikin ɗaya-da-ɗaya maimakon aiki na rukuni. Sassan shaida musamman na mutum ishe cewa mahalarta suna amfana daga keɓantaccen lokacin rubutu — da kuma tattaunawa ɗaya-da-ɗaya tare da shugabansa na coci lokacin duba abin da suka rubuta. Idan kuna aiki a rukuni, yi la’akari da ba mahalarta lokaci don aiki akan Kashi na 1 ɗayan ɗaya kafin zaman, sannan ka yi amfani da lokacin rukuni don duba da ƙarfafawa maimakon rubuta daga ƙasa.

Ga Amsa Tambayoyi a Kashi na 2, tambayoyin mafi kulawa na kiwo mai yiwuwa sune Q2 (baftismar jariri), Q6 (lalacewa bayan baftisma), Q10 (ƙin iyaye), da Q11 (ko akwai dalili mai kyau don kar a yi baftisma). Ka kasance shirye don ba wa kowane daga cikinsu lokaci mai faɗi idan mahalarcin ya ɗaga su da kansu — amsoshin da aka rubuta suna farawa ne, ba cikakken martanin kiwo ba.

Tambayar Ruhun Sama (Q1) na iya haifar da amsoshi masu ƙarfi a yanayin da ake koyar da tauhidi ’albarkar biyu’ ko ake ɗaukar shi a gaba. Ka kasance a fili kuma ka kasance da tabbas a amsar ka, amma ka guji raina kwarewa ta ruhaniya ta gaske na mutane. Manufar ba ita ce cewa ba su sami Ruhu ba — shine cewa sun karɓe shi a lokacin imani, ko da yake sanin aikin sa ya girma a tsawon lokaci.

Tambayoyin tunani na ƙarshe a ƙarshen Kashi na 2 — musamman gayyatar rubuta addu’a ta mutum — suna da muhimmanci. Kada ka yi hanzari a wucewa. Mahalarcin da zai iya rubuta addu’a mai gaskiya game da baftismarsa ya yi wani abu na gaske. Ka ɗauki lokaci don yardawa da shi.

Bayan kammala wannan babi, mataki na gaba yana da amfani: gyaran kwanan wata da farawa shirye-shirye masu ganuwa. Ka tabbatar da cewa wannan tattaunawa ta faru a fili kafin mahalarcin ya bar zaman.

pastorals3.body:
Ɗertirgel 1 naftorante ko golle gooto-gooto naa kawtal. Ɗertirgel shahaada ko waɗaa ngam neɗɗanke haa nder kawtal ngal naftorata sirru tuma winndude — e haalde gooto-gooto bee ardiiɗo mum cokkal tuma ndaarta ko ɓe winndii. Ko waɗata nder kawtal, miijton waɗde ngam nder kawtal ngal Ɗertirgel 1 gooto-gooto ɓako zuwana, haa kawtal ɗon waɗu ɓaawo ngam ndaarta e ɓurtinde naa winndugo fuɗɗorde.

Ngam Jaabaaje Ƴameeje nder Ɗertirgel 2, ƴameeje ɓurɗe naannde kisal maa waɗde Y2 (batiisma jariŋŋaari), Y6 (yonaago ɓaawo batiismu), Y10 (baɗdi aynaaɓe), e Y11 (ko woodaa dalil naftoraniiɗo ngam de’ireede batiismu). Tiiɗon ngam waɗde waktu dow yii ɗe ko nder kawtal ngal neɗɗo umma ɗum tele — jaabaaje winndaaɗe ngon fuɗɗornde, naa jaabaawol kisal fow.

Ƴameede dow Ruuhu Seniiɗo (Y1) waɗata e jaabaaje tiiɗuɗe nder yonki to tinnditaare ’albaraka ɗimmonde’ ekkitineeɗo. Wonde teeŋtungol e tiiɗningol nder jaabaawol maa, kono taa cela kewkewe ruuhaniije goonga yimɓe. Ɗam-ɗam wonaa ko ɓe nannditaako Ruuhu — ɗum wonaa ɓe heɓii mo nder waktu imaan, ko waɗaa anndol kuugal mum ummi e waktu.

Ƴameeje miijitol ɓaawo nder ɓaawo Ɗertirgel 2 — ko waɗaa ngam winndol du’aawu neɗɗanke — teeŋti. Taa henyu. Neɗɗo nder kawtal ngal mbaawi winndude du’aawu goonga dow batiisma mum waɗii huunde goonga. Heɓ waktu ngam tinndinugo ɗum.

Ɓaawo kammude babi nde, golle teeŋtunde ɗon ngoni golle-gollanaango: tiiɗinde raawangu e fuɗɗude mbaadi yiylaade. Tiiɗon ko haalde ngon laati teeŋtude ɓako neɗɗo nder kawtal ngal yaltude zuwana.

watch1.body: 👁️ Watch for participants who complete Part 1 superficially — a vague date, a half-written testimony, a short guest list. Superficial preparation usually signals either unreadiness or unresolved anxiety. Either way, it deserves a follow-up conversation before the baptism proceeds.

watch2.header: Abubuwan da ya kamata a kula da su
watch2.body: 👁️ Ka lura da mahalarta waɗanda suka kammala Kashi na 1 a saman saman — kwanan wata mara tabbas, shaida da aka rubuta da rabin, jerin baƙi gajere. Shirye-shiryen saman saman yawanci yana nuna ko rashin shirye-shirye ko damuwar da ba a warware ba. Ko yadda yake, ya cancanta tattaunawa ta bi kafin baftisma ta ci gaba.

watch3.body: 👁️ Ndarna gite ma dow nder kawtal ngal nder kawtal ngal kammindooji Ɗertirgel 1 ɗun seeɗi — raawangu miijaangol, shahaada demi-winndaandi, liste baraaɓe seeɗi. Mbaadi haakirii wakkati feere hollitiima mbaadi wonaa tano walla sunaare gollaaɗo. Ko fuu waɗaa, haaji haalde ɓaawo ɓako batiismu waɗde.

paras: Jay Parsons is the Associate Minister at Chalmers Church, Edinburgh, UK

Jay oversees work with children and young people, membership and Global Missions at Chalmers.  He has a significant role in pastoral care and is part of the preaching team.  After studying at Oak Hill Jay worked for 10 years on the staff team at Christ Church Banstead before moving to Edinburgh in 2020.
Jay is married to Jo and they have three children. Jay is still holding onto the idea that he will one day play centre forward for Leeds United.

publisher.name: BeaconLight
paras: BeaconLight is a non-denominational Christian training organisation based in the UK, founded in 1995. Its aim is to equip Christians worldwide with practically-applied Biblical gospel ministry tools — resources designed to help people both equip themselves and equip others.

BeaconLight’s resources include CrossCheck, which enables Christians to explain their faith and lead others to Jesus, and Word@Work, a verse-by-verse applied devotional commentary of the whole New Testament. BeaconLight also provides printed Biblical resources to prisons, seafarers, police, and the armed services.
BeaconLight believes that Christian ministry should be free of charge, and all its online materials are available without registration or payment. The ministry is funded entirely by donations.
BeaconLight is a member of the Evangelical Alliance and Global Connections, and is a registered charity in England and Wales (Charity No. 1047046).
